title: Mandatory-compound and stop-count pressure
summary: Dry F1 races normally require at least two slick compounds, while special events can mandate more stops.
rationale: Dry F1 races normally require at least two slick compounds, while special events can mandate more stops. A driver late in the race without satisfying the likely compound/stop requirement has a strategic pressure to pit that generic tyre degradation features may miss.
implementation_hint: For each Year/Race/Driver sequence, compute cumulative unique dry compounds used so far, has_changed_dry_compound, stops_so_far from PitStop cumulative sum, laps_remaining, and rule-pressure interactions such as dry_no_compound_change_late and one_stop_only_final_third. Add Monaco/known special-race flags only if Race and Year support them.
expected_effect: Should help late-race positives and distinguish old-tyre stay-outs from forced stops caused by regulations or minimum strategy obligations.
risk: Wet/intermediate races suspend normal dry-compound logic, and special rules vary by year/race; incorrect rule flags can hurt if applied globally.
